steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: To the right of the blank, “celebrated el Día de los Tres Reyes Magos” is a clause with an implied subject. The blank must contain a noun phrase that will serve as the reference for this following clause.

steps.title: Apply the correct punctuation rule
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two independent clauses when they are closely related.

Here, describing the uncommon practice and stating what that practice celebrated are two distinct coordinate ideas requiring semicolon separation: “practice at the time; celebrated el Día de los Tres Reyes Magos”.

Commas alone (A, C, D) do not provide sufficient punctuation for coordinate clauses.

That is the correct answer.
